heading: Gibson Desert North vs Gibson Desert South — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gibson Desert North, Western Australia and Gibson Desert South, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gibson Desert North has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Gibson Desert South (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gibson Desert North or Gibson Desert South safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gibson Desert North has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Gibson Desert South scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gibson Desert North scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Gibson Desert South scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gibson Desert North or Gibson Desert South?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gibson Desert North has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
